Defines the built-in properties and routines for the ECMAScript Object.prototype.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines the built-in properties and methods of the `Object.prototype` in a JavaScript engine. It uses macros to declare object identifiers, object properties, and routine properties associated with the `Object.prototype`. The `OBJECT_ID` macro specifies the identifier for the `Object.prototype`, while the `OBJECT_VALUE` macro defines the constructor property with configurable and writable attributes. The `ROUTINE` macro lists several methods such as `toString`, `valueOf`, `toLocaleString`, `hasOwnProperty`, `isPrototypeOf`, and `propertyIsEnumerable`, each associated with a C function and specifying the number of arguments and the length property value. The file concludes by undefining the macros to prevent conflicts with other code.
